content:
That is my server. It' s sometimes difficult to get the bosses on board on some of my plans within this server. It may be a slight barrier language but I think they are coming around.

I am trying my best to get you guys the best experience. So please, give me time 🙏

https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1354754985119846581/1433472586955755550
I think this post should have included a redirect to <#1400750050208125010>
Many don't know this channel exists
Also <#1400750050208125010> should be moved next to <#1170692781216378960> to improve visibility
They should switch places
Nvm I agree with that idea

imo, Chinese and English should have their own category regrouping their content all into one place with similar structure (General, Off-topic, Help, Guide and the likes) while the rest of the languages can be grouped together. It's a matter of usage, and whenever one more language becomes a lot active then separate and create their own category, but as long as they're small and not very active, they can have only 1 channel. Arguably spanish could have a bit more channels or their own category because they're decently active enough from what I saw from afar
Even the Guide page have very little visibility as it is currently

some people raised a point that the old english-chat channel was never labeled to not be a general chat channel until now, and many in the community has settled around it to be what it was; so why not put it to a vote how many in the community wants it to be changed/reorg'd? if, instead of just one or two new comers losing their way from newcomer-help, the majority of people been playing and supporting the game for years would like to keep it as it was, it should count for something no?
This is on the Agenda. 👍
It is what I'm focusing on but to do this I will need everyone to grab their corresponding "language" role. This is a long project and it takes time. I have done this with other servers in the past.
It will not happen overnight. So bare with me

I've been on Discord for around almost inception, and I've also been in multiple 6 or 7 digit member servers.
My genuine concern is this server is actually impossible for people to talk about the game, and here's why.
1. Multiple language channels that aren't hidden behind roles, or overall server channel management
In larger servers, many language chats are hidden behind roles so people aren't swamped by the amount of options offered. This may be the developer's first Discord, so I'll give them leeway, but once your server becomes 50k or potentially even 100k members, this will 100% become an issue you will face. Even if it's not a problem for a large amount of players currently, this will cause confusion for newcomers that will leave.
2. Overspecialized channels for both Morimens and Non-Morimens related content
I'll be extremely honest here, Morimens is a game with certain depth, however, at its current level of complexity, we don't need that many channels.
There's understandable channels, like guide threads or a dedicated fan art one. The issues come from the other channels. New player threads are something I don't fully agree with, because new players often end up with confusion how to use them and their common questions fall into a format a moving chat format can address better. Traphase? Team building? Story Discussion? Even finding this channel on my sidebar is a struggle...
My suggestion is, you can probably merge a lot of the existing channels into a singular Morimens channel if they're redundant (especially stuff like Lore, helpbuilds, and Traphase since it is relevant main content in the game) and keep a few specialized channels for things like guides and art so people don't need to scroll to find specific ones for that.
3. Defining channel purpose
This ties back in to my 2nd problem, the overspecialized channels problem. I'm here because of 1 main reason, I've been a 2 month regular on the English chat, now currently the Morimens chat. [Continued]

But the actual Morimens chat...doesn't have much to talk about. What do you have left for it on-topic wise when the other channels are there? Memes? Pulls?
No, the real reason it's there is basically *for lost new players*.
Which honestly shouldn't be a problem, if we restructured it so new players can jump straight into the new player chats with role selection too.
Yes, you can setup roles for that too...
Also, since previously the chat was an EN based chat, the sudden change in dynamics have caught many regulars astray, and I'm not entirely opposed to the changes here.
I spent this time writing this because I do care about the state of this server, and I don't have any ill intentions. I know it might not be all nice things to say, but I do think they need to be said because they are problems with the current server, especially organization.
I stopped reading at 67 <:Cheerup:1400771555939844149>
From my experience 90% of the time questions about traphase and lore got ignored in general chat so redirecting them to the specific channels is good imo <:A_pinch:1432980538536493057>

I strongly disagree with the suggestion to merge the morimens channels - lore discussion and gameplay discussion are **significantly** different, and the majority of the participants in the general channel either do not care about story/lore or are actively dismissive of it
passing by to just say it's nice to see casual next to morimens-chat, it should work decently for the time being, until the final version

the participants of the story discussion channel are active enough as a group even if there is not 24/7 activity constantly going on in the channel, and trying to fold that into a general all-encompassing channel would be fairly destructive to the group as most of us do not participate as much in general chat (and I personally much prefer the slower and more on-topic discussions we have in the story channel)
I agree keep lore channel and eng-chat separate
I actually agree though, but I do like to counter that general chat is the main gateway to how newcomers and story skippers learn the lore.
If you want that channel to be more active, I suggest the devs promote it when joining. <:point:1356053112292511815>

I strongly disagree with merging traphase.
It is a different game. In traphase channel, you can see ppl say pandia op and it makes perfect sense.
But if you merge everything, it will get confusing fast
But, I can also say that this server is a mess, and we only have 29k people. <:point:1356053112292511815>
What does that have to do with anything?
Because it's only going to get more messy with more members later on, not just now
It's an actual problem
well...general chat is a mess! story discussion is doing quite fine, imo <:karen_nervous:1408324291237511259>

We can cross that bridge when we get to it.
I will admit, the channel placement and categories are weird

As somebody who never used the story channel but sometimes discuss or bring up lore topics in eng-chat. There's only like so few people who actually engages with lore topics there. Not to mention there's some people who hates on certain awakeners that prompts newcomers to hate them too both lore and gameplay. Also i prefer a steady chat than one who constantly changes topic in a heartbeat. So keeping both separate is ideal
though yes I do agree that better categorization and channel order would be much appreciated

when you find yourself in a hole, the first thing you do is to stop digging

merging the channels would make it more messy
qwq
You know what, why don't we ask the people who join? Not just us regulars.
feel free to come talk in the story channel!! we love discussing the lore <:Cheerup:1400771555939844149>
Like my question is, if any of us were new players, would you think they could find the channels they're looking for in this current layout?
Or, they'd jump straight to the Morimens chat and then get backlinked instead?
They'll probably miss channels due to current structure but will get redirect by the ppl at <#1170692781216378960>
Then be fine
You see, the redirection is a bandaid solution
The thing is, if it's organized, you wouldn't need to do that
So it's 100% a problem
You'd go from point A to B, not A to B to C

I believe the existings channels are fine, but it's all spread and inside categories that require to be added manually, and roles. Admin said it's a work in progress so we'll see the end result, but for now I'd rather see the category reorganisation before considering what to remove
I did mention on the very top a lot of it requires role setting
It's about hiding irrelevant chats from people who won't go to them
If you've been around Discord on mature servers, a lot of them bot or role the unnecessary channels out of the way
Yes, that definitely takes time, but it's necessary
I like changes now
Everything is clear and close together
Can't complain
It will get better.

There should be seperate pings for server news, and stuff directly dealing with the game or just making an announcement channel for certain stuff. Some pings seem a bit unnecessary or gets lost when posted in like a general chat with a bunch of people sharing opinions.

Maybe a channel or topic forum or thread for specific bugs or fixes. Then another for official art or comics and announcement.

 People can easily see stuff than doing all that scrolling
A couple of English Vc's could be made in English chat. To just homogenise the discord a lil. We could leave the global category entirely.
